Hey, welcome back. Are you ready to talk about ads and stuff? That's what we're gonna be doing for the rest of our time together and the more we do this, the happier you're going to be that we just spent the last bit of time working on making sure your sales process is dialed in, making sure your emails and stuff are all set and making sure that super system is starting to get built out because that really does make ads better. Now look, this video, this one right here that you're watching, may very well be the single most important video on the topic of advertising and this one is really how to think properly about ads and I know it's like counterintuitive for me to say this is the most important thing, but I obsessed over making this particular part of your training over the past month. Like this has been driving me nuts because I'm like, man, I really want to do a good job with this. I want to convey this information to everybody. You know, I really want them to grasp this and I was like, you know, should I make a big fancy presentation? Should I make it animated or something to to maintain people's attention? I finally decided on I would make this video for you as if I were making it for my children who one day might want to get into advertising and I might not be around. So that's like how serious this is. This is if I had one thing to teach my kids about ads and that was it and I could never teach them anything else because like I was gonna go to Mars or something, you know, or maybe my time's up. You never know. It's not like I'm a health nut, you know. This what I'm about to share with you. This is what I would teach some. Above all, like here's how to write a headline, above all, like here's how to do copy, above all, like here's how to do bullet points, any sort of position, anything like that. So what we're gonna cover in this is really how to think like a true successful advertiser because no kidding, 99% of success in ads is here. And I don't mean that in like, if you know me, like, you know, I'm not a, you know, woo woo third eye, you know, kind of person, nothing wrong with that stuff. But I'm more like, okay, show me the bottom line, show me the numbers and everything. And so for me to come out and be like 99% of this game is mental, that's a really strong statement. And it is 100% true. All right. So here's the thing about ads. I'm just going to talk to you like I'm talking to my kids, you know, and I'm not going to have to say don't hit your sisters, but I'm sure whatever. All right. So here's the thing about ads. Number one, the purpose of advertising is to multiply money. Period. Okay. And we are going to take as a given that the ads are truthful. Okay, we're going to take it as like, duh, that the ads, you know, or that your product lives up to its promise. And we're going to take it as a given that you're nice to people. So like, that's obvious, you know, so if you were to say to someone, hey, the number one thing you need to do on earth is to like be kind to your fellow man or something. Like we're taking it as a given that you also need to breathe and eat regularly and stuff like that. I think you'd get it. Right. So the whole point of ads is to literally take a dollar and turn it into $2 or $3 or $4 as quickly as possible and as much as possible. While still being a good person and still being cool and not lying and cheating and stealing or anything like that. So that means, all right, that ultimately at the end of the day, the most important thing about ads is ROAS. Return on ad spend. That's what that stands for. It's so important to me that I have it tattooed on my freaking hands. Right. Like that's it. That is what it's all about. Return on ad spend. And here's why I'm starting here. Right. So the logical question will be, how do you get a good return on ad spend? And like, that's really not that hard. There's moving pieces, but it's just doing the fundamentals really, really well. But the reason I'm starting with, you know, the thing is, that's it right here. This, the reason I'm starting with this is because, you know, I've been working with entrepreneurs 22 years every week. I talk to clients and every week I hear the same thing, which is, you know, I want to talk to you about my campaigns. All right. And I say, OK, tell me what's going on. And they'll say, well, I think this is what's going on. And I feel like this is what's going on. So here's the deal in the world of ads, there is absolutely no room for I think or I feel. There's no room for this should. There's no room for this could. The only thing that exists in the world of ads is these are the numbers. All right. And so at the end of the day, the whole world of advertising, it's ultimately a math game. M-A-T-H math, mathematics, not to be confused with illicit substances. Right. It's ultimately just about numbers. It's about math. Now, personally, I've always hated math. I did terrible in school at math. I was a straight A student until third grade, and then my grades started slipping and math was the first one that it slipped in. You know, I did not technically graduate from college. My math teacher, my senior year, it was college algebra, even though I was in high school. I technically didn't graduate from high school. That teacher upgraded my grade from a failing grade to a C minus because we both were fans of the Grateful Dead. So I'm not telling you you need to be a math wizard. Like all of the calculations you need to do can be done, you know, on your phone, right? On your calculator. It's nothing complicated. But my point is, are you getting more than you're spending? And are you paying attention to the math? So it's a math thing. And so if you take a step back from there, the thing that's like critically important and this is just as important, if not more important than the actual ad itself, and that is the data. All right. So when it comes to running ads, whether it's Facebook, direct mail, YouTube, you know, by the way, in this training, we're going to deal with Facebook, Instagram, YouTube, and Google Display Network and omnipresent retargeting, omnichannel, omnipresent retargeting. So like all that stuff, fundamentals always apply, right? But at the end of the day, you have to have the right data because the data, you know, here's how much I spent. Here's how many clicks I got. Here's my cost per click. That gives you the accurate math, which helps you figure out your return on ad spend, right? Your row has. So at the end of the day, it's never about how many impressions I got. It's never about how many clicks I got. It's about, I put this much in. I got this much out. And that's it. Right. And so the reason I tell you that it's mental is because your brain, if you're anything like me, and if you're anything like every other client I've ever had ever, your brain is going to do a lot of stuff that's totally counterintuitive and totally counterproductive. So number one, your brain, my brain, everyone, it will assign a level of probability to the success of the campaign. That is directly proportional to how much of a pain in the asset was to build everything. Right. So if you and I build a sales process and, you know, let's say it takes like four weeks or something to build this thing out. We got nine billion emails and this landing page and all this kind of stuff. And then, you know, we, we take another week scripting the perfect ad and finding the perfect targeting and all of that. And we turn the ads on. Our brain is going to subconsciously say, okay, we worked really hard on that. So the, the chances of success for that campaign, really, really high. And so if it doesn't work out, our brain is going to act irrationally and let us do dumb stuff. Instead of just going, oh, the numbers are saying that's not working. Let's do something else. Right. This is why this is so critical because despite everything, we will attach this should work. I feel like I think we'll attach all that kind of stuff to our ads and we'll end up losing money. All right. And so the whole, again, the whole purpose of advertising is to make money like as quickly as you can while being a good person. All right. So data, data is king. Accurate thinking is king. Right. So like if you can just be a cold hearted, you know, no emotion robotic person for 30, 45 minutes a day, then you're going to be fine. The minute you start thinking, okay, this should work or shouldn't work or this should be this way or I think or you're unclear on numbers and stuff, you're dead. Right. And so no one's going to tell you that, by the way, because it's not sexy. It's not fun. Like, hey, let's take a class from Frank Kern about math. You know, that's, I'm not going to sell a lot of those. But at the end of the day, that's, we're really going to be doing a lot of just looking at the numbers, making sure they're right, adjusting things accordingly. Now here's another thing. The platform doesn't matter. All right. So all the platform is like, and by a platform, I mean, I mean, Facebook, YouTube, Snapchat, Instagram, you know, just pick one, Google display, Google search, YouTube. I think I said that already. The platform's irrelevant. Right. So you're going to get all kinds of information about this is where the money is or this platform's where the money is or this platform's where the money is. No, it's not. All the platform is, is a way of like delivering your words and pictures to somebody else on their phone, generally speaking, in this day and age, like right on their phone or on their computer. That's all it is. It's just a way of sticking a message in front of somebody. Right. So the platform, it's important to know how to use them all. You want to use every single one of them you can. But if you like accidentally fall down the rabbit hole of thinking, I'm going to just do this one ad platform, then you're going to get all the money. You're going to lose money. You could like directly lose money or you could lose money in the sense that you're making less than you could if you were on other platforms. So that's going to happen if you fall for that trap. Right. So if you really think about it, we got a couple of things that we really, really, really need to agree on, which is this number one. All right. It's all about cash in cash out. That's it. Right. Again, we're taking it as a total 100% given that you're not lying, cheating, stealing and that your stuff is good. Like, duh. So with that considered, that's all it's about is return on ad spend. I put a dollar in, I'm getting more than a dollar out. Number two, everything's about math. And the math, by the way, in ads and stuff, it's really like nothing complicated. You know, there's only a couple of things we really look at. So like in a traditional funnel, you're like, okay, am I getting leads? Or am I getting visitors to my sales page? And am I getting sales? And am I making money? Right. And so if you're not making money, you're just going to look at like one of those things, you know, and so the only stuff that could go wrong, going to be like, well, you're paying too much per click on your ad or people aren't opting in or they're opting in and they're not buying from your sales page. You know, and so like, there's not a lot of moving pieces with this stuff once you understand it. But dude, if you don't have the metrics and the data, you are dead. And so nobody ever wants to talk to you about it because it's boring. And that's why I'm making such a big deal out of it now. I'm hoping like you're watching this here and be like, okay, I'm going to remember this as we go forward. You know, because especially if you're new to this, you know, you're going to be like, man, this is a pain in the ass. This really should work. And then it'll cause you from, it'll keep you from turning off and add the sucks and replacing it with one that is that works. And you know, for all we know, it's the picture. Or it's the copy in the ad or it's like one little thing, you know, so we've established that like that row as is everything. We've established that it's really all about math and that we're not really doing complicated calculus here. You know, it's just the stuff that I discussed that you need to measure. And we've established that there is zero room for emotion in this. Okay, nothing. And we've established that there is no one platform. Like we want to use everything. Why wouldn't we? That's crazy. You know, ads are nothing but a combination of words and pictures displayed to somebody. That's all an ad is. So a platform is irrelevant. As long as we can do that, we're going to be okay. You know, so what does it take? Like what do you actually need in order to be good at this kind of stuff? And by being good, I don't mean winning awards and stuff. I mean, putting the dollar in and getting more than a dollar out within an acceptable timeframe and doing it at scale. Like what do you have to have? Well, the first thing you have to have is rational thought. Right. So you really have to think about everything just like as if you are the most boring, you know, like anal retentive, completely devoid of personality, robotic human being. That's how you need to think. Just when you're analyzing your ad campaigns, you can be creative and crazy stuff the rest of the day. But when you're looking at how my ads do it, you need like hyper rational thought. Like turn every other part of your brain off other than, okay, I spent this much. I made this much. Is this acceptable? If not, okay, where is it broken? It's broken here. What needs to be changing? I will test this. And this is the course of action I'm going to take. That's it. Right. The next thing you need is a steady hand. Okay. So here's the thing. I'll give you like a concrete illustration of this in a minute. What you see in webinars and what you see in internet marketing ads and stuff where a person will spend like 500 bucks in a day and they'll make $1,000 back in a day is cool. Right. But that's not where the money is. The money is in like, I'm going to spend 500 bucks today. And what is that investment going to bring me in the next 30 days or in the next 90 days? Right. So what happens because you're going to see stuff and you have seen stuff where like, I've even shown you stuff where like, Hey, man, look, I spent this and I made this and this is great. You know, and it'll cause your brain to be jumpy. It'll cause your brain to be like a, like a squirrel. Like, you know, squirrels are like freaked out. Generally speaking, if you're going to Georgia, that's where I'm from. You know, there's squirrels everywhere and they're the most nervous creatures in the world. You know, they're constantly, what was that? Dude, what? What? What? You know, I'm going to like, what do they talk about? They're like a bunch of speed freaks or something sitting around. Do you hear that? I hear what? I don't know. You know, but it causes our brain to act like that. So the first thing by steady hand, I mean that you need to be able to launch a campaign, whether it was just say Facebook for now, because it's up there when it's familiar with the end of like launch a Facebook campaign and get, let's say, a hundred visitors and be able to have nothing cool happen and not freak out and change everything. All right. So that's the second thing there. So I can't tell you how many of these conversations I've had where I'll talk to someone and I'll say, okay, I watched a campaign. I got 23 clicks and four people opted in. Nobody bought yet. What do I do? And like, it's to the point where, you know, I'm not even polite now when I answer the question, which I shouldn't be impolite, but I get so frustrated. Like you have to understand that we're dealing with billions of people on the planet that are seeing your stuff, you know, and you can just like saying, I have a storefront and 23 people walked by the store and a couple of people came in, but nobody bought anything. Should I change the entire store? Should I go get a new lease in a different location? Should I like fire sale all the inventory and go from an ice cream store to a barbershop? You know, what should I do? All based on that one little tiny sliver of a couple of people walking by and poking their heads in and not buying anything. So a steady hand means you got to like really hold fast and not be panicky. OK, now here's what's weird. The reason I bring this up is because something our brains do. OK, our brains can like get us really excited and they can really get us really depressed basically over nothing. I'll give you an example. Let's say you run a campaign today and you're like, I'm going to test a campaign today, I'm going to spend $100 and you make zero sales. What's your brain going to do? Well, let me let me phrase the question the opposite way. Let's say you run a campaign today, you spend $100 and you get back a thousand. What's your brain going to do? Your brain is going to say, holy crap, I just figured out how to make $1,000 a day. I'm set for life on the opposite spectrum. If you run an ad, spend 100 bucks, get zero sales. Your brain is going to say, oh my God, I'm going to lose $3,000 a month doing this. I can't live like this. This is horrible. I got to change everything. All right. No, you don't. You got to look at the data and be like, okay, do I even need to change anything? I only spent 100 bucks. What am I paying for a click? You know, I'll show you this stuff to analyze, but you got to realize that advertising, it's not, if you look at every single day, you know, your ads are going to be like, yay boo, yay boo, yay boo, like that's life. You know, some days are great, some days are horrible. Generally speaking, you just take like a seven day average and look at everything, make sure it's cool and make your decisions based on that. You know, so you got to have that steady hand. All right. So rational thinking, steady hand. Those are the first two things. The next two things, hopefully you've gotten because we spent a lot of time on them in the beginning and that is the right sales process, right? And good funnel optimization. Remember, so we spent a lot of time on Super System. We spent a lot of time on sales process. We spent a lot of time on the checklist of, hey man, here are all the things you could do to maximize the amount of sales that you're going to get from any traffic sent from ads. The fact of the matter is, as you're about to witness, it's not hard to drive traffic to a web page. It's like, it's not a big deal. You know, the challenge is in getting those people to buy stuff. And while the ad itself is important, right, like you're not going to have a lot of success if your ad says, you know, moderately good food served by relatively clean convicts who have been out for at least a day and haven't even killed anybody yet. Right? Like that's probably not going to get a good reception for trying to drive people to a restaurant, but generally speaking, it's just, you know, it doesn't really matter. Like as you see, as you're going to see when we first start testing ads, we always use really basic copy. We use two sentences like, read this if you want this big result, like anybody can drive traffic to a web page. The challenge is turning those visitors into customers and turning those visitors into money. And in order to do that, you got to have a good sales process that you really optimize and you got to have, you know, a super system and good follow up and all that kind of stuff. So those are the next two things. Right? Now, the final thing you got to understand is that Facebook, Instagram, like the internet, OK, like every advertising platform that's digital, not necessarily like direct mail or anything, but every digital platform, it's smarter than us. Thank God. All right. That means it's got algorithms, it's got machine learning, it's got artificial intelligence. So if you can have a steady hand, all right, it will actually be able to go out and start finding customers for you without you having to obsess over audiences. This is another thing that I'm bringing this up because I've worked with so many clients, I'll see people and they'll be like, OK, I'm only going to show this to people that are between the age of 42 and, you know, 59 and they're divorced and they're female and they live in the United States and they sell insurance and they'll try to narrow their target market down perfectly. OK. And so anytime you do that, it doesn't matter what the platform is, let's say it's Facebook, anytime you do that, you're automatically spending more. OK. But if you sell stuff and the average person who buys it is a lady who's divorced, who's between whatever age I said and who's got interests and insurance and all that kind of stuff and you just say show it to everybody or show it to a whole bunch of people and not an audience at Nero. Let's just say Facebook, the platform, the machine learning algorithm, regardless of platform, it'll actually go out and just find that person, but it'll do it for less because you're not telling it to charge you a premium for filtering down the audience so much. So you always want to understand that machine learning and AI, it's your friend, it's pretty good. All right. So this is like how to think like an advertiser. Now, let me drive this home with some good old fashioned math. OK. All right. So here we are on my computer. I'm using an app called HiRos, by the way, which I really, really love. It's a great Facebook and it's really like all ads, attribution, tracking software is not mandatory. I've made it for 20 something years without having it, but I just got it recently and I absolutely love it. And so I'm filming this for you on the 11th of August. And I just realized it's like seven million degrees in my office because I don't want to have to yell over the background noise of my air conditioner. So if I look just like a sweaty freak today, it's not because I'm a sweaty freak. It's because it is a gazillion degrees in here. Anyway, look at today. All right. So like today, and I wish I'd film this video for you earlier because this was actually losing money a couple of hours ago. But now we're making money. All right. But my I've spent seven hundred thirty nine bucks and I've made a profit of one hundred thirty three dollars, which is like I'll still take it. You know, it's better than a poke in the eye. Right. And so if we look down here and this is all going towards like, I want you to think long term about your ads. If we go down here, we can see like, you know, this one, for example, like this, this is a classic example. I spent a hundred ninety three dollars on this audience. OK, so all of these are on this campaign, rather, all of these are campaigns that are selling the same thing. I'm going to be using this a lot for teaching. This is my four dollar bundle of stuff and it's got some upsells. And no, I haven't built a cool super system for it, like the one that I showed you how to build, but I should. So this is what that's for. Right. So for today, for this particular campaign, this one usually does really well. I spent a hundred ninety three bucks and I've only brought in a hundred forty. So I've lost money. All right. My average order value was seven dollars. Now, most people would think, oh, man, you should turn that off. Right. But the first thing that we want to do in advertising, regardless of the platform, is understand long term thinking and its benefits. So this is, of course, you know, our return on ad spend is very, very important. We always want to make money, but it's pretty rare that you're going to make a ton of money if you try to make it all back in the same day. If you give yourself more time to make money, then you're going to make a lot more. All right. And I'll prove that to you in a minute. So let's look at this one. This is the actual campaign. And if I click down in here, it shows you my ad sets. Now, this one right here, this is where I'm targeting literally every single human in the world this over eighteen and speaks English. I don't know if there's ever been a day that this is actually lost money, but I've spent one hundred sixteen dollars just targeting everybody today. And it's lost twenty one dollars. So like a lot of people would freak out over here, but this is what you need to understand. OK, so let me look at now. I'm going to show you my report for the week. OK, just for like the past seven days. Where's my date range? Here we go. OK, so I'll just do like past seven days here. All right. And let me show you what a seven day window looks like. You can understand. And see for yourself this whole concept of not getting to freaked out over things not working. OK, so let's look at where is this is the campaign now. Look now over the over the last seven days, this campaign has made money. Now it's lost money today, but over the last seven days, it's made money. Now, look at this thing. Literally everyone. All right, I've spent a total of four ninety two on this sucker. And it's made me a net profit of a hundred or rather fourteen hundred dollars. But look, these other ones, these other ones have lost money. Now, if I was letting the squirrel brain run the show, I might freak out here. OK, but I know my numbers. All right. And so let me show you something really, really important. Let's look at the past 30 days. I'm shooting this for you on the 11th. OK, so let me go back here and I'll say custom range from July 11th until August. So technically that's more than 30 days, but whatever the 11th to the 11th. All right. This is the really, really important thing to look at. And if you're just starting with ads, you're not going to know these numbers yet. So you're going to have to guess. OK, but this I'm taking you down this level of a rabbit hole for a very specific reason, which is I want to save you mental anguish and I want to make you a lot of money. If we look at this, this sits from January or January, July 11th until August 11th. My gosh, my brain is on the word now. We can see I've spent 31 grand. I brought in 61 grand of net profit of 30 G's is pretty good. Right. And I built all of these campaigns for the sole purpose of teaching this stuff. So these are my teaching campaigns. I really limit myself in doing the campaigns the way that I'm doing them. Because in reality, I will make more money if I go negative. Now, here's why. All right. So look at this number right here. This is A O V. So this means this is the average amount of money that a customer spends with me in their initial purchase setting. Right. So this means that as long as I get a customer, generally speaking, for twenty six or for under twenty six dollars, I'm going to be as a general rule profitable. Right. Now, sounds good. Sounds easy. But this is short term thinking. Now, remember, I showed you like that super system that I built, right? With the one I always make fun of. And I'm like, I bubble gummed and duct taped this thing together and is selling like nine million different things. And I just kind of slapped it together. Let me show you why even having a crappy one like that is better than having nothing at all. All right. So we know, all right, like all of these guys, this is all these ads right here. They're selling one thing, which is my four dollar bundle and it's got some upsells. And then they're in the super system and their first, the average individual, they're worth twenty six dollars at the time of purchase, you know, with the upsells and all that kind of stuff right there at the time of purchase. Now, check this out. All right. If I come over here to this report and I go over here and like, I'm kind of giving you a crash course on high rows, but I just want to show you this data. All right. If I run a report and I say, OK, show me the lifetime customer value of every customer where the first thing they ever bought for me was this and give it to me over like 30 days. Right. So that's about now. Right. You know, so from the data I just gave you. Now, remember my average order value was what, twenty six dollars or something? I think. Watch this. OK. This doing his magic here. Is working in stuff. It's making squiggly lines. You can't have too many squiggly lines. All right. Do do do do do do. There we go. I almost had to pause the camera. Look at this. The average customers actually were thirty one dollars and fifty cents because they buy more stuff. All right. And that's just over a 30 day period. I've only been using this campaign as a teaching campaign for literally like since July 1st or something. So I don't have data for longer than 30 days. But this number is only going to go up. What this really means is that, hell, even if I paid twenty seven dollars to acquire a customer over a 30 day period, I can still make money because of the super system. Now, why would I show you that? Right. Because I want to show you the way this stuff really works. Like I'm literally for these campaigns, I'm showing you because I use them for teaching and because I want to be able to show a positive return on ad spend all the time, I'm literally killing stuff that would make me more money long term because it's not very exciting to be like, all right, here's a campaign that lost some money. But look, if you wait 30 days, it made a whole lot like no one's going to get excited over that. But I've got the data right here. I know, right? So like looking at the screen, I know, OK, man, these dudes are worth, you know, thirty one dollars and fifty cents just over 30 days. I know this number is going to change the longer I run this stuff, but still, I'll take this. So, man, even if I lose a dollar per sale, I know I can wait it out and still make over 10 percent return on my ad spend over 30 days doing nothing, just letting the super system happen. And the more leads and customers I get, of course, the more money I'm going to make over the long term. See, this is what I mean by you got to think really rationally. You got to get rid of all emotion. You got to get rid of any attachment, any funnel, any campaign, any ad, any audience, any opinion, and you just have to let the numbers do the thinking. And the more data you have and the more accurate data you have, the better all of this stuff is going to be for you. All right. So that's how to think like an advertiser. So your mission is really to determine how much you're willing to pay to get a customer within the next 30 days. Right. And if you have a hunch, let's say you're new and you're selling something, but you know, you're going to be selling something later and like the next 30 days, you're going to have another webinar or whatever. Then you've only got like a $10 offer right now. If you're new, you're going to have to guess. You're going to be like, well, I think my customer is going to be worth, you know, $15 over the course of 30 days or $20 or whatever. I want you to come up with that number because that's what we're going to be looking at in the long term is am I getting customers for above this number or for below that number? Right. And so once you actually figure that stuff out and you turn off all the noise and you turn off the distraction and you get rid of the emotion, you realize, man, this is all we're really doing. We're just going to be showing words and pictures to people on devices. And then we're just looking to see which combination of words and pictures and people give us the most money. And we're just kind of doing more of that. And we're doing less of the stuff that doesn't work. That's literally how advertising work. All right. But if you don't have these fundamental beliefs in place and you don't have a steady hand and you're not making decisions based on math and you're not getting the right data and you're not focusing on ROAS, then you're dead. All right. And I don't want you to be dead. I want to be rich and I want you to be happy. I want you to have a lot of fun and want to be healthy. It's real hard to do any of that stuff if you're stressed out and freaked out. So think like a true advertiser. Everything else is gravy if you get these principles down. OK, enough of the mental stuff. Now let's get into actually running.
